response:
Let’s check if \( 16249 \) is prime.  

First, test small primes:  
- Not divisible by 2 (odd).  
- Sum of digits: \( 1+6+2+4+9 = 22 \), not divisible by 3.  
- Doesn’t end in 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5.  
- Check 7: \( 16249 \div 7 \approx 2321.285... \), not integer.  
- Check 11: alternating sum \( 1 - 6 + 2 - 4 + 9 = 2 \), not divisible by 11.  
- Check 13: \( 13 \times 1249 = 16237 \), remainder 12, so no.  
- Check 17: \( 17 \times 955 = 16235 \), remainder 14, so no.  
- Check 19: \( 19 \times 855 = 16245 \), remainder 4, so no.  
- Check 23: \( 23 \times 706 = 16238 \), remainder 11, so no.  
- Check 29: \( 29 \times 560 = 16240 \), remainder 9, so no.  
- Check 31: \( 31 \times 524 = 16244 \), remainder 5, so no.  
- Check 37: \( 37 \times 439 = 16243 \), remainder 6, so no.  
- Check 41: \( 41 \times 396 = 16236 \), remainder 13, so no.  
- Check 43: \( 43 \times 378 = 16254 \), too high, so no.  

We need to check up to \( \sqrt{16249} \approx 127.5 \).  

Let’s try 47: \( 47 \times 345 = 16215 \), remainder 34, so no.  
Try 53: \( 53 \times 306 = 16218 \), remainder 31, so no.  
Try 59: \( 59 \times 275 = 16225 \), remainder 24, so no.  
Try 61: \( 61 \times 266 = 16226 \), remainder 23, so no.  
Try 67: \( 67 \times 242 = 16214 \), remainder 35, so no.  
Try 71: \( 71 \times 228 = 16188 \), remainder 61, so no.  
Try 73: \( 73 \times 222 = 16206 \), remainder 43, so no.  
Try 79: \( 79 \times 205 = 16195 \), remainder 54, so no.  
Try 83: \( 83 \times 195 = 16185 \), remainder 64, so no.  
Try 89: \( 89 \times 182 = 16198 \), remainder 51, so no.  
Try 97: \( 97 \times 167 = 16199 \), remainder 50, so no.  
Try 101: \( 101 \times 160 = 16160 \), remainder 89, so no.  
Try 103: \( 103 \times 157 = 16171 \), remainder 78, so no.  
Try 107: \( 107 \times 151 = 16157 \), remainder 92, so no.  
Try 109: \( 109 \times 149 = 16241 \), remainder 8, so no.  
Try 113: \( 113 \times 143 = 16159 \), remainder 90, so no.  
Try 127: \( 127 \times 127 = 16129 \), remainder 120, so no.  

We’ve checked all primes up to 127, none divide 16249.  

Therefore, **16249 is prime**.  

**Answer:** yes
